preamble: The shift from [o] to [e] is a two-step process involving fronting (i-umlaut) followed by unrounding. While Standard German retains the rounded [ø], many dialects and English (a close relative) completed the shift to [e].

examples.note: Yiddish unrounded MHG ö [øː] → [eː]. All German front rounded vowels were unrounded: MHG hören → Yiddish hern (to hear), MHG schön → Yiddish sheyn.
Unrounding of the standard German umlaut.
